_comment: SpotMeter mod - configuration. v6.1.0+: the PRIMARY home is %APPDATA%/Wargaming.net/WorldOfTanks/mods/spotmeter/spotmeter.json (auto-created; survives modpack reinstalls). <WoT>/mods/configs/spotmeter.json still works as a legacy fallback and is migrated to AppData on first load. NumLock-on, NumpadPeriod (or Delete) reloads in-battle. Most common options are also editable in-game via the mods-settings menu (Aslain's aslainMenu or izeberg's ModsSettingsAPI) when installed.
_comment_schema: Schema version. v1 (no field) = pre-v6 flat config; v2 = v6.0+ with defaultToggles + auto-pick. Loader auto-migrates v1 -> v2 in memory; this file is only rewritten when you save via the v6 in-garage menu.
_comment_language: UI language: 'auto' reads the WoT client language (pl -> Polish, anything else -> English). Force with 'pl' or 'en'.
_comment_view_mode: If true (default), use the player's own view range as the assumed enemy VR. If false, use enemyViewRangeFallback. Picker (when active) overrides both.
_comment_crew: Approximate crew Camouflage skill multiplicative bonus for the player's tank.
_comment_color: Circle colors as RGB int. 16737095=tomato (moving), 3329330=lime (still), 16753920=orange (afterShot), 2263842=forestGreen (camo net active).
_comment_circle_switch: v6.1.0: master switch for the minimap spot-distance circle. false = panel-only mode (e.g. when XVM's own circles are enough). Editable live from the mods-settings menu.
_comment_perf: How often to recompute (s) and the speed (m/s) above which counts as moving.
_comment_fire: After firing: camo *= invisibilityFactorAtShot for fireRevealDuration s.
_comment_camo_net: Camo net on the player's own tank activates after camoNetActivateSec of NOT MOVING and adds the descriptor's invisibilityBonus. Auto-detected from optionalDevices.
_comment_picker_layout: Numpad layout for picker. NumLock-on or off both work. Defaults: 8/2 cycle enemies, 5 clear; toggles for VR computation: 7 Rations (ON), 3 BIA (ON), 4 Recon+SitAware (ON), 1 Directives (OFF), 0 Field Upgrades BETA (OFF). 9 toggle live mode, * dump enemy descriptor to log, NumpadEnter snapshot, NumpadPeriod reload config. Auto-detected equipment from descriptor: Coated Optics, Stereoscope.
_comment_picker_bonuses: v5.6 two-stage VR model. Stage 1 - crew amplifier from base_vr: crew_amplified = base_vr * (1 + (Rations? 0.0430 : 0) + (BIA? 0.0253 : 0)). Both Rations and BIA compute against base_vr, NOT against each other. Stage 2 - additive bonuses from crew_amplified: each of optics/stereo/Recon+SitAware adds crew_amplified * (factor - 1). Equipment factors (optics, stereo) come from descriptor; Recon+SitAware bundle is the toggle. Directives multiply equipment factors by 1.025 when ON. Field upgrade applies to base_vr first (with cap), then both stages run.
_comment_field_upgrades_BETA: BETA. Per-tank field-upgrade VR % for tanks that have a VR-related upgrade in their post-progression tree. Applied multiplicatively to base_vr, capped at pickerFieldUpgradeCap (445m). Tanks not listed get 0% (= no effect). Add your own entries by their shortName. Server doesn't transmit vehPostProgression for enemies, so this is a manual lookup. EBR 105 has no VR upgrade.
_comment_optics_vents_cvs_levels: WoT 2.x server doesn't transmit enemy optionalDevices, so optics / vents / CVS never show up in the decoded descriptor. Optics + vents are 5-level cyclable presets (0=OFF, 1=basic, 2=basic in specialty slot, 3=Bonds (red), 4=Deluxe (purple)); CVS is 3-level (0=OFF, 1=zwykly, 2=na slocie) because CVS has no Bonds/Deluxe grade. Numpad 6 cycles opticsLevel, Numpad + cycles ventsLevel, Numpad - cycles cvsLevel. Optics percentages: +10% / +11.5% / +12.5% / +13.5% (VR multiplier). Vents percentages: +5% / +6.25% / +7.5% / +8.5% (multiplies the additive crew bonuses rations / BIA / recon+SitA). CVS factors below 1.0 reduce OUR moving camo when the picked enemy has CVS: zwykly 0.900 (-10%), na slocie 0.875 (-12.5%). Tune any of the three arrays if needed.
_comment_overlay: v5.5: status-block model. Block shows spot distance for ALL 4 states at once (ruch/postoj/siatka 3s/po strzale) plus picker+toggle+own-tank context. Two ways to see it: (1) NumpadEnter = on-demand snapshot; (2) Numpad9 = toggle live mode (default OFF) which re-posts the block every liveModeIntervalSec seconds. NO auto-spam on state changes / radius deltas. User-action confirmations (toggle/picker/diag) are short one-line responses, not the full block.
_comment_auto_pick: v6.0: continuously track the closest visible enemy as VR target. Default OFF. Toggle in-battle with NumpadSlash. Manual pick (Numpad 2/8) overrides; Numpad 5 clears manual and (when auto on) restores auto. Position cache holds last-known fix for autoPickCacheTimeoutSec so the target doesn't flicker when a spotter blinks. Range is 2D ground distance from your tank.
_comment_auto_presets: Per-class presets applied ONLY while auto-pick is ON. Set ONCE when you ENABLE auto (toggle off+on to re-apply), based on the class of the tank auto picks at that moment - does NOT re-apply as auto retargets. Numpad presses override live. Keyed by WoT class tag (lightTank/mediumTank/heavyTank/AT-SPG/SPG); 'default' is the fallback for classes without their own entry. Levels 0..4 (0=OFF 1=basic 2=slot 3=bonds 4=deluxe). Default: lights = optics+CVS on slot + rations/BIA/recon ON; everyone else = optics+CVS OFF + rations/BIA/recon ON.
_comment_defaults: v6.0: which picker bonuses start ON at the beginning of each battle. Applied ONCE at WoT startup; in-battle Numpad toggles change the live state without altering these defaults. Hot-reload of this file does NOT re-apply (so a Numpad7 mid-session won't get flipped back). Restart WoT to pick up changes.
_comment_default_levels: Multi-level cyclable controls: opticsLevel + ventsLevel + cvsLevel. Each 0..4 indexing into the matching pickerXxxFactors array. Defaults: optics=4 (Deluxe, what most high-tier players run), vents=0 (OFF, less universal), cvs=0 (OFF, CVS is rare). Numpad 6 / Numpad + / Numpad - cycles them in battle; they reset to these defaults at the start of each battle.
_comment_menu_button: v6.0: floating SpotMeter menu button in the hangar. Drag with the mouse to reposition; position persists here. Coords are absolute pixels from screen top-left and are clamped to the visible area at load, so changing resolution won't make it disappear. Defaults aim at the bottom-of-hangar area to the right of the customization brush icon at 1920x1080.
_comment_panel_toggle: Hotkey to show/hide the battle panel (v6.1.0: battle-only - the garage panel is gone). Default PageDown (KEY_PGDN). This frees KEY_PGDN from being the NumLock-off alias of Numpad3/BIA (Numpad3 still does BIA). battlePanelEnabled below sets STARTUP visibility; this key flips visibility live and can summon the panel even when the flag is false. Set to "" to disable the hotkey.
_comment_panel_keyset: v6.1.0: optional multi-key combo for the panel toggle, written by the mods-settings configurator (list of Keys names, e.g. ['KEY_LCONTROL','KEY_PGDN']). Empty = single key from panelToggleKey. Triggers on the LAST key; the others must be held.
_comment_battle_panel: v6.0.0: in-battle SpotMeter picker panel rendered via the bundled GUIFlash fork (spotmeter_gf - shipped INSIDE this .wotmod, NO external gambiter.guiflash needed). Lists enemies (grouped if battlePanelGroupSameTanks), shows target spot-distance + AUTO state + toggle/level state. Pick with Numpad 2/8 or by clicking a row; 5 clears; 1/3/4/7/0 + 6/+/- for toggles & levels; NumpadSlash for auto-pick. Drag to reposition (coords persist here). PageDown shows/hides it.
_comment_panels_default_off: v6.1.0: panels start HIDDEN by default (modpack feedback). PageDown still summons them; set these to true for the v6.0-style always-on behaviour - or just tick the boxes in the mods-settings menu.
_comment_group_same_tanks: If true (default), identical enemy tanks collapse into ONE panel row and ONE Numpad 2/8 cycle stop, labelled 'Name xN' (same model = same view range = same spot circle, so listing each separately just means cycling through identical circles). Set false to list every enemy individually.
_comment_auto_hide: v6.1.0: battle-only - hide the panel while a scoreboard key (battleHidePanelKeys, TAB/N) is held; restore on release. false = panel always on top.
_comment_battle_hide_keys: In battle, hide the panel while one of these keys is held (TAB/N show the team-stats overlays); released -> panel returns. WoT Keys names.
_comment_garage_panel_gone: v6.1.0: the garage panel was removed - its settings (panel visibility, loadout defaults, hotkey) live in the in-game mods-settings menu now. Old garagePanel* keys in existing configs are ignored.
_comment_debug: logCalcDetails=true logs camo/radius/state each tick to python.log.
